ucs.title: [LLM] admin@338 (G0018) cmd.exe-driven host-triage burst — canonical recon command sequence
ucs.description: Hunts admin@338's signature post-implant 'host triage' — within minutes of LOWBALL/BUBBLEWRAP/PoisonIvy landing on a victim, the operator pushes a hardcoded sequence of native discovery commands (ipconfig, systeminfo, net view, net localgroup, net user, tasklist, netstat, schtasks) through cmd.exe. The detection counts how many distinct recon binaries one host runs in a 10-minute window and alerts when ≥4 fire. This bursty recon-by-shellscript pattern is documented by FireEye for admin@338's financial/economic-policy targeting campaigns.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command_lines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_recon_bins from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","query.exe","schtasks.exe","route.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where distinct_recon_bins >= 4 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// admin@338 (G0018) — hardcoded host-triage recon burst via cmd.exe
let TriageWindow = 10m;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","query.exe","schtasks.exe","route.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
| extend ReconTag = case(
    FileName =~ "ipconfig.exe", "ipconfig",
    FileName =~ "systeminfo.exe", "systeminfo",
    FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "view", "net-view",
    FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "localgroup", "net-localgroup",
    FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "user", "net-user",
    FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "group", "net-group",
    FileName =~ "tasklist.exe", "tasklist",
    FileName =~ "netstat.exe", "netstat",
    FileName =~ "schtasks.exe", "schtasks",
    FileName =~ "route.exe", "route",
    FileName =~ "nltest.exe", "nltest",
    FileName =~ "whoami.exe", "whoami",
    FileName =~ "query.exe", "query-user",
    FileName =~ "hostname.exe", "hostname",
    "other")
| where ReconTag != "other"
| summarize DistinctRecon = dcount(ReconTag),
            ReconSet     = make_set(ReconTag),
            FirstSeen    = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen     = max(Timestamp),
            CmdLines     = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
            Parents      = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            Grandparents = make_set(InitiatingProcessParentFileName, 10)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, TriageWindow)
| where DistinctRecon >= 4   // empirical threshold — admin@338 burst observed at 6-8 in FireEye samples
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// admin@338 (G0018) — host-triage recon burst (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let TriageWindow = 10m;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","query.exe","schtasks.exe","route.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildName in (ReconBins)
| extend ReconTag = case(
    ChildName == "ipconfig.exe", "ipconfig",
    ChildName == "systeminfo.exe", "systeminfo",
    ChildName in ("net.exe","net1.exe") and CommandLine has "view", "net-view",
    ChildName in ("net.exe","net1.exe") and CommandLine has "localgroup", "net-localgroup",
    ChildName in ("net.exe","net1.exe") and CommandLine has "user", "net-user",
    ChildName in ("net.exe","net1.exe") and CommandLine has "group", "net-group",
    ChildName == "tasklist.exe", "tasklist",
    ChildName == "netstat.exe", "netstat",
    ChildName == "schtasks.exe", "schtasks",
    ChildName == "route.exe", "route",
    ChildName == "nltest.exe", "nltest",
    ChildName == "whoami.exe", "whoami",
    ChildName == "query.exe", "query-user",
    ChildName == "hostname.exe", "hostname",
    "other")
| where ReconTag != "other"
| summarize DistinctRecon = dcount(ReconTag),
            ReconSet  = make_set(ReconTag),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            CmdLines  = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            Parents   = make_set(ParentProcessName, 10)
            by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, TriageWindow)
| where DistinctRecon >= 4
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: FireEye's admin@338 reporting documents the same handful of recon commands (ipconfig /all, systeminfo, net view, net localgroup administrators, net user, tasklist /v, netstat -ano) being executed within seconds of implant install — the operator's tradecraft cookbook, not opportunistic reuse. Counting ≥4 distinct discovery binaries from the same user/host inside 10 minutes is highly specific to scr

ucs.title: [LLM] admin@338 weaponised Office attachment → child process from user-writable directory
ucs.description: Hunts the admin@338 spearphishing-to-implant handoff: a Microsoft Office process (Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook) or Equation Editor (eqnedt32.exe) spawning cmd.exe / scripting hosts / LOLBins, OR spawning ANY child whose image lives in %TEMP% / %APPDATA% / %ProgramData% / C:\Users\Public. This is the canonical detonation moment for admin@338's CVE-2012-0158-class RTF lures and macro-bearing decoys themed around financial / trade / economic newsworthy events that drop PoisonIvy and Bubblewrap.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command_lines values(Processes.process_path) as image_paths values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe") AND ( Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe") OR Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*") ) AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// admin@338 (G0018) — Office/EQNEDT32 weaponised attachment detonation
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let SuspChildren  = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe"]);
let WritablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
| where FileName in~ (SuspChildren)
   or FolderPath has_any (WritablePaths)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent       = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          Child        = FileName,
          ChildPath    = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256  = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// admin@338 (G0018) — Office/EQNEDT32 weaponised attachment detonation (Sentinel 4688)
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let SuspChildren  = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe"]);
let WritablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend ChildName  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentName in (OfficeParents)
| where ChildName in (SuspChildren)
   or NewProcessName has_any (WritablePaths)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          Parent     = ParentProcessName,
          Child      = NewProcessName,
          CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: admin@338 Office/EQNEDT32 Spawns Implant Child Process
id: a3380018-0001-4338-b338-adminat338001
status: experimental
description: Detects Microsoft Office or Equation Editor processes spawning command-shell / scripting / LOLBin children, or spawning any child from user-writable directories. Canonical admin@338 (G0018) spearphishing-attachment detonation pattern dropping LOWBALL / BUBBLEWRAP / PoisonIvy.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0018/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/china-based-cyber-threat-group-uses-dropbox-for-malware-communications
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0018
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_office:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
  child_susp:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\hh.exe'
  drop_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
  condition: parent_office and (child_susp or drop_path)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate macro-driven business workflows (rare; should be allowlisted by signed publisher)
  - Office add-in installers writing to AppData
  - Click-to-Run repair operations
level: high
ucs.rationale: admin@338 is documented (FireEye / MITRE G0018) as primarily a spearphishing-with-malicious-Word-attachment actor, leveraging CVE-2012-0158 and macro lures themed around economic / financial / trade newsworthy events to drop PoisonIvy, LOWBALL and BUBBLEWRAP. The attachment-detonation pattern is identical across the cluster: Office (or eqnedt32 from RTF OLE objects) hands off to either cmd-driven
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
